title: viburnum opulus subsp. typicum
intro: Viburnum opulus subsp. typicum, also known as the guelder rose, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its berries, leaves, and bark, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus subsp. typicum include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions made from its leaves, berries, and bark, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
